zh.lifeSummary: 當Joseph Chapman於1740年6月14日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州弗雷德里克時，他的父親Giles Chapman Jr.38歲，母親Sarah H Jackson30歲。他於1810年之後與Catherine Anderson結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1816年在美國南卡羅來納州紐貝利享年76歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joseph Chapman于1740年6月14日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州弗雷德里克时，他的父亲Giles Chapman Jr.38岁，母亲Sarah H Jackson30岁。他于1810年之后与Catherine Anderson结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1816年在美国南卡罗来纳州紐貝利享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Chapman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a merchant or trader, Middle English chap(pe)man, chepman, Old English cēapmann, cēpemann, a compound of cēap ‘barter, bargain, price, property’ + mann ‘man’.
Jewish: adopted probably for a like-sounding or like-meaning name in some other European language; see for example   Kaufman  .

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. John Chapmen (sic) was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Kaufman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Chaim, Emanuel, Moshe, Eytan, Herschel, Zvi, Ari, Aron.
Americanized form of German   Kaufmann  , and a variant of the same Jewish surname.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Kaufman meaning ‘merchant’. Compare Koyfman 2.

variants.name: Chipman
meanings: English: from Middle English chipman (Old English cȳp(e)mann), a side form of chapman, chepman ‘merchant, trader’; see Chapman , with which this name was interchangeable.

variants.name: Copeman
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): patronymic from the early Middle English personal name Cop(e)man, an Anglicized form of Old Scandinavian Kaupmaðr. It is a transferred use of Old Scandinavian kaupmaðr (Old Danish køpman) ‘merchant’, cognate with Old English cēapmann (see Chapman ), but there is no evidence for the occupational term in Middle English.
Americanized form of North German   Koopmann   or Dutch   Koopman  .

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

historicEvents.description: "Patrick Henry made his ""Give me Liberty or Give me Death"" speech in Richmond Virginia."
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: South Carolina becomes the 8th state in the Union
historicEvents.description: On May 23, 1788, South Carolina ratifies the Constitution of the United States making it the 8th State of the Union.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

en.lifeSummary: When Joseph Chapman was born on 14 June 1740, in Frederick, Virginia, British Colonial America, his father, Giles Chapman Jr., was 38 and his mother, Sarah H Jackson, was 30. He married Catherine Anderson after 1810. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1816, in Newberry, South Carolina, United States, at the age of 76.

Il a épousé Catherine Anderson après 1810. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1816, à Newberry, Caroline du Sud, États-Unis, à l’âge de 76 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Chapman(이)가 1740년 6월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프레더릭크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Giles Chapman Jr.(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah H Jackson(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1810년 이후 Catherine Anderson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1816년 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 뉴베리에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Chapman nasceu em 14 de junho de 1740, em Frederick, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, Giles Chapman Jr., tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Sarah H Jackson, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine Anderson após 1810. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1816, em Newberry, Carolina do Sul, Estados Unidos da América, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joseph Chapman родился 14 июня 1740 г. в местности фредирикк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Giles Chapman Jr. было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah H Jackson, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Anderson после 1810 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1816 г. в местности нюбирри, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joseph Chapman nació el 14 de junio de 1740, en Frederick, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Giles Chapman Jr., tenía 38 años y su mamá, Sarah H Jackson, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Catherine Anderson después de 1810. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1816, en Newberry, Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 76 años.
